channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Jimmy Carter, at age 100, casts his 2024 ballot by mail
item.description: PLAINS, Ga. (AP) — Jimmy Carter cast his ballot in the 2024 election Wednesday. The former president voted by mail, the Carter Center confirmed in a statement. It happened barely two weeks after Carter celebrated his 100th birthday on Oct.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: What to know about shaken baby syndrome as a Texas man could be first in US executed over it
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A Texas man this week could become the first person executed in the U.S. for a murder conviction tied to the diagnosis of shaken baby syndrome.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: People with felony records can now vote in Nebraska — and it could help tip the balance in November
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — Nebraska’s top election official had no authority to strip voting rights from people convicted of a felony, the state Supreme Court ruled Wednesday in a decision that could add hundreds of new voters to the rolls and potentially help tip the balance on Nov. 5.
item.dc:creator: MARGERY A. BECK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Harris holds 2-point edge in tight North Carolina race: Poll
item.description: Vice President Harris holds a narrow, 2-point lead over former President Trump among likely voters in North Carolina, according to a poll released Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota school district bans officer from teaching after he reenacted George Floyd's murder
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A Minnesota school district has banned a police officer from working as a substitute teacher after a series of “racially harmful” actions that officials say included putting a student on the ground for a reenactment of the 2020 murder of George Floyd by a Minneapolis officer.
item.dc:creator: BY STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Menendez brothers' family to push for their release as prosecutors review 1989 case
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The extended family of Erik and Lyle Menendez will advocate for the brothers' release from prison during a news conference set for Wednesday in downtown Los Angeles as prosecutors review new evidence to determine whether they should be serving life sentences for killing their pare
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New election rules in Georgia are in flux with voting underway
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — With less than three weeks to go before Election Day, the fate of new rules that could impact the certification of vote tallies in the critical swing state of Georgia is still in flux as judges wade through court challenges.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal money to help states hit by hurricanes Helene and Milton nears $2 billion
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal government has approved nearly $2 billion in assistance across six states to help people hit hard by hurricanes Helene and Milton, the White House said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: US Navy fighter jet with two on board crashes during training in Washington state
item.description: The U.S. Navy searched Wednesday through mountainous terrain for two crew members who were aboard a fighter jet that crashed in Washington state during a routine training flight. The EA-18G Growler jet from the Electronic Attack Squadron crashed east of Mount Rainier at about 3:23 p.m.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Arctic fox found stranded in Portland now receiving medical care
item.description: “While we don’t have information yet on where the Arctic fox came from, or how she got to Portland,” Bird Alliance of Oregon spokesperson Ali Berman said.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, NewsNation

item.title: Former Las Vegas-area official sentenced in murder of journalist
item.description: The former public administrator was accused of killing Jeff German after the reporter wrote articles critical of his conduct in office.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Bleakley, KRON4

item.title: Honey prices increase as Rio Grande Valley bees struggle
item.description: "It's very challenging with all the new problems we’ve had from imported pests and diseases," says Brownsville beekeeper Glenn Simpson of Bay Bees RGV.
item.dc:creator: Blake Reagan Brown, NewsNation

item.title: Man accused of threatening FEMA workers in North Carolina called on others 'to join'
item.description: The man says he believed workers were withholding supplies, but soon discovered that wasn't the case.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Ratliff, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: After 36 years, accused killer in Oregon cold case to stand trial
item.description: An autopsy revealed Deborah Atrops was physically assaulted and strangled to death. She was 30. Then, the case went cold.
item.dc:creator: Tim Steele, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: ISIS-K orchestrated foiled Election Day terror plot: US official
item.description: Afghan native Nasir Ahmad Tawhedi, 27, was arrested in early October and accused of planning to target people on Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Listeria recall grows to 12 million pounds of meat and poultry, some of it sent to US schools
item.description: A nationwide recall of meat and poultry products potentially contaminated with listeria has expanded to nearly 12 million pounds and now includes ready-to-eat meals sent to U.S. schools, restaurants and major retailers, federal officials said.
item.dc:creator: JONEL ALECCIA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Montana man camping near Big Sky found dead in tent after ‘vicious attack,’ police say
item.description: A man, later identified as Dustin Kjersem, 35, was found dead in a tent last weekend near Big Sky, Montana, and investigators are calling the case a homicide.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Clint Eastwood's daughter arrested in Beverly Hills in domestic violence case
item.description: Actor Francesca Eastwood, daughter of legendary actor Clint Eastwood, was arrested in Beverly Hills over the weekend on suspicion of felony domestic violence, according to reporting from the Los Angeles Times.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Navy: Fighter jet with two on board crashed in Washington state during training
item.description: The U.S. Navy was searching for two aviators who went missing after their plane crashed during a routine training flight in Washington state.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Secret Service trained at mock White House that Hollywood producer Tyler Perry built as stage
item.description: The U.S. Secret Service used Tyler Perry's mock White House in Atlanta for a training scenario in 2023 as it works to construct its own White House training facility.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Do you keep money in payment apps? Here's why you probably shouldn't
item.description: As the use of payment apps has grown in recent years, the Consumer Finance Protection Bureau has issued guidance on best practices to avoid pitfalls.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Arrest made less than 24 hours after Nashville woman was shot to death while walking on popular trail
item.description: Authorities have taken a man into custody in connection to the fatal shooting of a Nashville woman that was found Monday night inside a park.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Coca-Cola recalls 'zero sugar' lemonade after it's found to contain full sugar
item.description: The recall, which Coca-Cola issued in September but didn’t announce publicly, applied to 13,152 cases, each with 12 cartons of 12 cans of 12 ounces of lemonade.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: Amish family of 11 hospitalized after eating toxic mushrooms
item.description: A Pennsylvania family of 11, including nine children, were all hospitalized after ingesting toxic mushrooms Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, NewsNation

item.title: FEMA resumes door-to-door visits in North Carolina after threats tied to disinformation
item.description: ASHEVILLE, N.C. (AP) — Federal disaster personnel have resumed door-to-door visits as part of their hurricane-recovery work in North Carolina, an effort temporarily suspended amid threats that prompted officials to condemn the spread of disinformation.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA and SARAH BRUMFIELD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court turns away self-proclaimed Republican’s defense in claiming party’s support
item.description: The Supreme Court turned away a failed Minnesota state Senate candidate’s challenge to rulings that he falsely implied to have the Republican Party’s support during his 2022 write-in campaign.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Convicted killer Susan Smith charged after speaking with filmmaker
item.description: GREENWOOD, S.C. (WSPA) - Convicted killer Susan Smith has been convicted of a disciplinary charge weeks before her parole hearing. Smith, of Union County, was sentenced to life in prison for the murder of her children in Oct. 1994. The SC Department of Corrections confirmed with 7NEWS that on Aug.
item.dc:creator: Nikolette Miller, WGN 9

item.title: Massive meat recall includes hundreds of products sold at Walmart, Target, more
item.description: Check your fridge and freezer: A massive meat recall linked to possible listeria contamination has impacted hundreds of ready-to-eat meals sold at major grocery store chains across the U.S., including Walmart, Trader Joe's and Target.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, WGN 9

item.title: Walgreens to close 1,200 US stores
item.description: The plan announced Tuesday includes the closing of 300 stores that had been approved under a previous cost-cutting plan.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Man armed with rifle accused of threatening FEMA workers in North Carolina
item.description: A man accused of threatening federal disaster workers in western North Carolina has been charged, according to the Rutherford County Sheriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Ratliff, WGN 9

item.title: FEMA temporarily pauses aid in parts of NC over reported threats
item.description: The Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) temporarily paused aid in parts of North Carolina this weekend after alleged threats targeted the agency’s personnel aiding in recovery efforts following Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Major home improvement retailer files for bankruptcy, announces sale plans
item.description: True Value, a well-known home improvement company, entered into a sale agreement early Monday morning after filing for Chapter 11 bankruptcy.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, Fox 5

item.title: Bath & Body Works apologizes for candle designed with what looked like KKK hoods
item.description: Bath & Body Works apologized for selling a winter-themed candle that came under scrutiny for label imagery that drew comparisons to Ku Klux Klan hoods, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Eggnog before Thanksgiving? Shoppers scramble store calendars
item.description: Holiday merchandise traditionally started showing up in stores mid-October, and holiday offerings ramped up starting mid-November.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What $35 trillion in US national debt means for Americans
item.description: With the national debt standing at more than $35 trillion, economists fear that the country's debt load will only grow.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, WGN 9

item.title: Nearly 200 miles of Blue Ridge Parkway reopens as long-term work remains in North Carolina
item.description: The Blue Ridge Parkway was closed indefinitely after Hurricane Helene swept through the region last month.
item.dc:creator: QCN Digital Staff, Fox 5

item.title: What you should do before and after your phone is stolen or lost
item.description: Phones hold so much of our digital lives — emails, social media and bank accounts, photos, chat messages and more — that if they ever get stolen or go missing, it can cause major disruption
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
